steps.title: Read the plotted values
steps.content: The relevant recorded temperatures are $(1,69)$, $(2,60)$, $(3,73)$, $(5,64)$, $(6,62)$, and $(7,65)$.

steps.title: Compare the changes
steps.content:
Compute each listed change.
$$x=6\text{ to }x=7:\quad 65-62=3$$
$$x=5\text{ to }x=6:\quad 62-64=-2$$
$$x=2\text{ to }x=3:\quad 73-60=13$$
$$x=1\text{ to }x=2:\quad 60-69=-9$$

steps.title: Choose the greatest increase
steps.content: The greatest increase is $13$ degrees, from $x=2$ to $x=3$. The answer is C.
